1) Remove “AI-generated” labeling everywhere
What you already found
deck-views.tsx has something like:
description: `AI-generated deck: ${aiTopic}`
Replace with brand-safe copy
Pick one of these (my recommendation is #1):
Option 1 (neutral, clean)
description: aiTopic ? `Study deck: ${aiTopic}` : "Study deck"
Option 2 (premium positioning)
description: aiTopic ? `${aiTopic} — Exam Prep Deck` : "Exam Prep Deck"
Option 3 (no topic in description at all)
description: "Study deck"
Also search/replace globally
Search for:
"AI-generated"
"AI Generated"
"Generated by AI"
"AI deck"
"AI:"
…and remove/replace with brand copy. You already started this search — keep it as a batch.
2) Themes: make theme selection apply consistently (and persist)
You mentioned you located THEMES array. Common issues:
theme applies in one component but not the canvas/doc
theme doesn’t persist per deck/product
theme switch doesn’t restyle existing blocks
What to implement
Add themeId to deck/product model (DB)
Save theme changes immediately
Apply theme at render-time (not “baked” into blocks), where possible
Provide “Apply theme to existing content” for baked styles
If you already have applyThemeToDocument(doc, themeId) for the Product Builder, mirror the same pattern for decks.
3) DeckReportCard: make it actually useful + tied to study session saving
You said you reviewed:
DeckReportCard
study session saving logic
DeckHub delete button area
Typical fixes that unlock real value:
Save sessions reliably (no silent failure)
Show:
total reviews
accuracy %
current streak
cards mastered / learning
next review due (if you do SRS)
“Resume session” CTA
Implementation notes
Ensure POST /api/study-sessions (or equivalent) is called on:
session end
tab close (best effort)
after X cards (checkpoint)
DeckReportCard should read aggregated stats:
either compute client-side from session history
or better: server endpoint GET /api/decks/:id/stats
4) DeckHub delete button: safety + UX polish
Good delete UX = fewer support tickets.
Implement:
Confirmation modal with deck name
Soft-delete (optional) so you can restore
“Deleting removes study history” warning only if true
Disable button while deleting + toast success/failure
If deck is public/shared: require extra confirmation (“type DELETE”)
5) Port conflict in workflow: stop wasting time on restarts
If you keep hitting port conflicts:
make the dev server choose an available port
or ensure kill-on-restart
Typical Node/React fixes:
set PORT=0 or use Vite default
add a prestart script that kills old listeners (on Replit you can do a targeted kill)
ensure only one process (no double npm run dev)
If you’re using Vite, set server: { strictPort: false }.
One Replit AI prompt to batch this work
Copy/paste:
Batch implement these fixes and UX upgrades:

A) Remove "AI-generated" labeling
1) In deck-views.tsx (found around line ~120), change:
   description: `AI-generated deck: ${aiTopic}`
   to brand-safe neutral copy (e.g., `Study deck: ${aiTopic}`).
2) Search the repo for "AI-generated", "AI Generated", "Generated by AI", "AI deck" and remove/replace with brand-safe text.
3) Ensure no user-facing UI indicates AI generation unless explicitly required.

B) Themes consistency + persistence
1) Ensure THEMES selection applies consistently across deck views and persists per deck/product.
2) Add themeId to the deck/product record if missing, and persist updates to the server.
3) Ensure theme changes apply to existing content (either render-time theming or an "apply theme to content" pass).

C) DeckReportCard improvements (tie to study sessions)
1) Verify study session saving logic is reliable: save on session end + periodic checkpoints.
2) Implement a server endpoint GET /api/decks/:id/stats (or equivalent) returning:
   - totalSessions, totalReviews, accuracy, streak, masteredCount, learningCount, lastStudiedAt
3) Update DeckReportCard to display these metrics clearly and add a "Resume" CTA.

D) DeckHub delete button UX + safety
1) Add confirmation modal showing deck name and warning about effects (history removal if applicable).
2) Disable delete button while request in flight, show toast on success/failure.
3) Consider soft-delete if currently hard delete causes data loss issues.

E) Port conflict fix
1) Identify why multiple dev servers are running and prevent double-start.
2) For Vite, set server.strictPort=false and/or ensure process cleanup on restart.
3) Update workflow scripts to avoid port collisions.

Deliverables:
- PR-style code changes across the files you identified (deck-views.tsx, THEMES, DeckReportCard, delete button area, study session saving).
- Confirm in console there are no remaining "AI-generated" strings.
If you want the fastest “what to do next”
Apply the string replacement in deck-views.tsx immediately (tiny win)
Implement GET /api/decks/:id/stats and wire DeckReportCard (big perceived quality jump)
Add delete confirmation modal (reduces risk)
Fix port conflict last (unless it blocks you constantly)
If you paste the actual DeckReportCard file (or the endpoint names you currently have), I’ll give you a precise diff of what fields to return and exactly how to compute the metrics so it’s not guesswork.
